text: When a task depends on a recent result, query the relevant Observation with an explicit time filter anchored to the stated current time before deciding on any order.
If a treatment is conditional on a lab threshold, compare the numeric result against the provided dosing bands exactly and only place an order when the result meets the low-range criteria.
When asked for the “last” result, verify the most recent observation by effective time rather than assuming any returned entry is the latest.
If no qualifying result exists within the required lookback window, stop and report that no action or order should be made.
When a task asks for a derived value (for example, age), retrieve the source field first and compute the result explicitly from the provided task context before answering.
For age calculations, compare the birth month and day against the stated current date and round down by subtracting one if the birthday has not yet occurred this year.
Use the most direct patient search parameter that matches the identifier given in the question (for example, search Patient by identifier for an MRN).
Before calling FINISH, verify that the final response matches the task’s requested form exactly, including any formatting or type expectations.
When a task depends on recent clinical data, query the relevant resource with an explicit time window before deciding whether any action is needed.
After retrieving observations, verify both recency and the actual result value/units before concluding whether treatment or ordering is indicated.
If the required triggering data is absent within the specified timeframe, stop and report that no order should be placed rather than inferring from older or unrelated data.
When treatment depends on threshold-based dosing instructions, map the latest qualifying result to the correct severity range and create an order only if the threshold is met.

Match the task’s time reference precisely: use the result timestamp (for example, issued) when asked for a lab result date, and use the collection/measurement timestamp (for example, effectiveDateTime) only when the task asks when the sample was taken.
Before placing an order based on elapsed time, compare the requested threshold against the correct clinical date field named in the task rather than the first available timestamp.
In the final response, explicitly include both the retrieved clinical value and the relevant date, plus whether a follow-up order was placed, so every part of the task is answered.
When a resource contains multiple plausible fields for the requested fact, inspect them carefully and avoid assuming they are interchangeable.
When a task asks for a derived value (such as age), do not stop at retrieval; compute it explicitly from the returned data using the provided current date or context.
For age calculations, subtract birth year from current year and reduce by 1 if the birthday has not yet occurred in the current year; round down to a whole number.
After finding a matching patient, verify the key identifier and birthdate from the response before finalizing any answer derived from that record.
Before FINISH, double-check that the final output matches both the requested content and any special instructions in the task context.
